ArchitectureNotes.objective: Replace the monolithic bot with a contract-first GRACE-governed platform where the Go API owns orchestration and Python workers own heavy execution.
ArchitectureNotes.document-role: This artifact is the GRACE execution and architecture backbone for the migration. It is intentionally detailed enough to replace the original long-form markdown plan rather than merely summarize it.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not preserve a long-lived legacy compatibility layer and do not move heavy ML or report logic into Go.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Cross-surface contract drift between API, workers, Web UI, Telegram, and MCP can invalidate large implementation waves if contracts are not frozen first.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Retry, cancel, and webhook delivery can look correct in happy-path demos but regress under partial failure unless they are modeled before code extraction.

section.note: The role of the migration plan as implementation SSOT is preserved.
Current monolith shape, files, storage model, and preserved capabilities are preserved.

section.original: 3. Non-Goals for the First Platform Version
section.note: All explicitly excluded scopes are preserved.
All platform rules and separation principles are preserved.
All fixed migration decisions remain explicit and immutable until superseded.
High-level components and control-plane versus execution-plane split are preserved.
Target repository structure is preserved.
Compose services, network assumptions, and CPU-only whisper shape are preserved.
Core entities, job types, statuses, artifact kinds, and delivery strategies are preserved.
All operational tables, important fields, constraints, and rules are preserved.
Queue design, payload shape, retries, and SkipRetry rules are preserved.
Buckets, object keys, access model, and presigned download behavior are preserved.
Request rules, endpoints, delivery model, idempotency, and error codes are preserved.
Endpoint, event envelope, subscription model, and polling precedence rule are preserved.
Claim-run-finalize lifecycle, internal endpoints, and worker-specific progress models are preserved.
API rules, worker rules, and subprocess handling are preserved.
Retry-as-new-job lineage model is preserved.
Responsibilities for Web UI, Telegram bot, and MCP are preserved.
Required pages, actions, and late-wave admin operations are preserved.
Required tool surface is preserved.
Thin adapter behavior and current UX preservation are preserved.
Required services, volumes, and CPU resource notes are preserved.
Per-service environment contract is preserved.
Current-file to target-module mapping is preserved.
Wave 0 through Wave 9, deliverables, files, and phases are preserved.
Contract, API, worker, UI, Telegram, and MCP verification plans are preserved.
All named risks and mitigation directions are preserved.
Completion criteria are preserved.
Recommended implementation order is preserved as historical guidance.
Do and do-not rules for future implementation sessions are preserved.

file.role: Telegram runtime, source extraction orchestration, attachment download, media-group buffering, callback handlers, status messages, and result delivery
Current application service for processing sources, writing job metadata, generating report artifacts, and running deep research
YouTube transcript fast path and Whisper fallback

preserved-capabilities.item: Telegram voice, audio, video, and document transcription
artifact reuse inside the current process

DecisionProvenance.decision: Separate HTTP API server selected as the single backend boundary.
Async job model selected rather than synchronous request-response execution.
Separate worker processes execute heavy work; API does not run transcription, report generation, or deep research inline.
Object storage chosen as MinIO in Docker Compose.
PostgreSQL chosen as the persistent source of truth for jobs and artifact metadata.
No auth model in v1 because the stack is local-only.
MCP remains a separate thin adapter rather than being embedded into the API process.
Sources are uploaded through the API in v1 rather than via direct MinIO uploads.
Initial platform version intentionally drops cross-request dedupe and reuse.
Report and deep research are modeled as distinct child job types, not stages within one mutable row.
Project remains self-hosted and local-first without quotas.
Simple React UI is required and lives as a separate monorepo app.
Everything is containerized under Docker Compose including CPU-only Whisper runtime.
Clients choose one delivery strategy per job, polling by default or webhook when explicitly configured.
asynq is the queue substrate.
Artifact downloads use MinIO presigned links rather than API proxying.
Multi-file upload through the API is required in the first version.
There is no legacy compatibility track; the repository is refactored directly into the target architecture.
Go API module granularity is fixed for the migration horizon as internal/api, internal/jobs, internal/storage, internal/queue, internal/ws, and internal/db.
Worker-control contracts remain explicit HTTP routes for the migration and first platform version.
Redis is reserved for asynq queueing only; event delivery is driven from persisted job events, WebSocket broadcast, and webhook dispatch inside the API.

component.role: Go HTTP API and WebSocket server
Python worker for report generation jobs
React UI for job creation, monitoring, and downloads
Thin Python bot adapter over the HTTP API
Thin MCP adapter over the HTTP API
OpenAPI definitions, JSON schemas, WS envelopes, webhook envelopes, and shared enums
Typed client for Web UI and MCP server
Typed Python client for Telegram bot, workers, and local internal tooling
Outbound callback delivery owned by the API server

control-plane-responsibilities.item: accept uploads and source creation requests
resolve delivery strategy polling or webhook for create requests
deliver outbound webhooks on job lifecycle changes
expose artifact metadata and MinIO presigned download links
enforce idempotency and child-job reuse semantics

execution-plane-responsibilities.item: emit progress updates back to the API

notes.note-1: The repository is restructured directly into the target monorepo shape.
notes.note-2: There is no long-lived legacy subtree and no compatibility branch inside the target structure.
notes.note-3: Existing Python modules may remain briefly while being actively moved, but the plan assumes direct replacement rather than prolonged coexistence.

InputShapes.shape: single-source transcription job backed by a one-item source set
combined multi-media transcription job backed by an ordered multi-item source set

rules.rule-1: Each job chooses exactly one delivery strategy.
rules.rule-3: Webhook can be requested only at job creation and requires a callback URL.
rules.rule-4: The API always returns job identifiers and initial snapshots even when webhook is selected.
rules.rule-5: Polling endpoints remain available as recovery and read access even for webhook-configured jobs.

Enums.enum: queued, running, cancel_requested, succeeded, failed, canceled
transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx, report_markdown, report_docx, deep_research_markdown, execution_log

Table.name: job_submissions
Table.purpose: Represent idempotent create requests including multi-file upload cases that cannot be modeled safely on jobs alone.

columns.column: created_at timestamptz not null default now()

rules.rule: One submission may create many jobs for POST /v1/transcription-jobs.
Same submission key plus same normalized request returns the original accepted response.
Same submission key plus different normalized request returns idempotency_conflict.

Table.name: sources
Table.purpose: Persist uploaded-file, Telegram-upload, and YouTube URL source metadata.

columns.column: created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: youtube_url rows require source_url and do not require object_key
uploaded_file and telegram_upload rows require object_key

rules.rule: YouTube sources may exist without MinIO objects in v1.
Sources are reusable input records; jobs reference source sets rather than direct source_id after the source-set migration.

Table.name: source_sets
Table.purpose: Represent ordered input sets for transcription jobs so single-file and combined multi-media flows share one model.

columns.column: created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: input_kind is constrained to single_source, combined_upload, or combined_telegram in v1 implementation space

rules.rule: Single-file transcription jobs use a one-item source set.
Combined transcription jobs use a multi-item ordered source set.

Table.name: source_set_items
Table.purpose: Persist the ordered list of sources that belong to one source set.

columns.column: source_set_id uuid not null references source_sets on delete cascade
source_id uuid not null references sources
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

rules.rule: position defines the final transcription order.
The same source may appear in only one position within one source set in normal API flows.

Table.name: jobs
Table.purpose: Represent root jobs, child jobs, retries, delivery strategy, progress, and terminal outcomes.

columns.column: root_job_id uuid not null references jobs
source_set_id uuid not null references source_sets
delivery_strategy text not null default polling
progress_stage text not null default queued
progress_message text not null default empty
params jsonb not null default empty object
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: webhook rows require webhook_url, polling rows do not carry webhook_url
indexes on root_job_id, parent_job_id, retry_of_job_id, source_set_id, job_type plus status
partial unique canonical child constraint by parent_job_id and job_type for non-retry active or completed rows

rules.rule: Initial transcription job has root_job_id equal to id, no parent_job_id, and no retry_of_job_id.
Retry jobs keep root_job_id and parent_job_id but set retry_of_job_id.
Report jobs point to transcription parents; deep-research jobs point to report parents.
Only one canonical non-retry child per parent and job_type exists in v1; reruns happen through retry rows.
Retries and child jobs reuse the same source_set_id as their parent lineage.

Table.name: job_artifacts
Table.purpose: Persist output artifact metadata after successful MinIO object writes.

columns.column: job_id uuid not null references jobs on delete cascade
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

rules.rule: source_original is not modeled as a job artifact in v1 because uploaded originals already live in sources.
Create rows only after object upload succeeds.
Transcription success requires transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, and transcript_docx.
Report success requires report_markdown and report_docx.
Deep-research success requires deep_research_markdown.

Table.name: job_events
Table.purpose: Append-only timeline and delivery trigger store keyed by job version.

columns.column: job_id uuid not null references jobs on delete cascade
root_job_id uuid not null references jobs
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: indexes on job_id ascending and root_job_id ascending timelines

rules.rule: Timeline events are append-only.
One job version maps to one persisted event row.
Webhook deliveries reference job_events rather than mutable job rows.

Table.name: webhook_deliveries
Table.purpose: Persist bounded webhook retry state across API restarts.

columns.column: job_event_id uuid not null unique references job_events on delete cascade
job_id uuid not null references jobs on delete cascade
state text not null default pending
next_attempt_at timestamptz not null default now()
created_at timestamptz not null default now()

constraints.constraint: indexes on state plus next_attempt_at and job_id plus created_at

rules.rule: Create one row per emitted job_event when delivery_strategy equals webhook.
No separate per-attempt history table is required in v1; attempt_count plus last outcome is operationally enough.

QueueContract.PayloadShape: Minimal payload is job_id plus attempt number. Full mutable job metadata must not be placed in asynq payloads.

OwnershipRules.rule-1: The API owns business retry semantics even though asynq performs transport retries.
OwnershipRules.rule-2: asynq retries are only for transient infrastructure failures.
OwnershipRules.rule-3: Deterministic business failures must use SkipRetry rather than blind queue repetition.

SkipRetryCases.case: claim rejected because job is already terminal or already owned
pipeline or model failure that should surface to the user as terminal failed

AccessModel.rule-1: Clients do not upload directly to MinIO in v1.
AccessModel.rule-2: API stores source files in MinIO.
AccessModel.rule-4: Clients receive MinIO presigned download links from the API and download directly from those links.
AccessModel.rule-5: YouTube sources may exist only as DB rows and worker-local temporary materialization in v1.

PresignedLinks.rule: Presigned URLs are generated by the API at read time and must be treated as ephemeral.
Job snapshots and artifact metadata remain available from the API even when URLs expire.

CommonRequestRules.rule-2: Create endpoints accept delivery strategy. If delivery strategy is webhook, a webhook URL is required.
CommonRequestRules.rule-3: Equivalent request plus same Idempotency-Key returns the original accepted response rather than creating new jobs.
CommonRequestRules.rule-5: Create-child behavior is stronger than idempotency: if a canonical child already exists, return it even without an idempotency key.
CommonRequestRules.rule-6: Multipart request normalization includes scalar fields and ordered file manifest with filename, size_bytes, and sha256.
CommonRequestRules.rule-7: Recommended first-version maximum request size is 1 GiB.
CommonRequestRules.rule-8: The existing upload endpoint preserves one uploaded file to one transcription job semantics; combined multi-media transcription uses a separate endpoint.

SharedEnums.enum: queued, running, cancel_requested, succeeded, failed, canceled
transcript_plain, transcript_segmented_markdown, transcript_docx, report_markdown, report_docx, deep_research_markdown, execution_log

Endpoint.purpose: Create one transcription job per uploaded file.

fields.field: files binary required one or more
display_name optional only when exactly one file is uploaded
delivery_webhook_url optional required when delivery_strategy equals webhook

Endpoint.response: 202 Accepted always returns jobs array, even for one file.

behavior.rule: Multiple uploaded files create one transcription job per file.
Uploaded files are persisted to MinIO before queueing work.
Reject empty file parts and ambiguous display_name usage for multi-file upload.

Endpoint.purpose: Create one combined transcription job from an ordered set of uploaded media files.

fields.field: files binary required one or more
delivery_webhook_url optional required when delivery_strategy equals webhook

Endpoint.response: 202 Accepted returns one job snapshot, not an array.

behavior.rule: API persists each uploaded file as its own source object.
API creates one ordered source set preserving multipart file order.
API creates one transcription job bound to that source set.
Combined flow does not change the semantics of POST /v1/transcription-jobs or the URL flow.

Endpoint.purpose: Create transcription job from supported YouTube URL.

validation.rule: Allowed hosts are youtube.com, www.youtube.com, m.youtube.com, and youtu.be.

Endpoint.response: 202 Accepted with the same job envelope shape as upload flow.

Endpoint.purpose: Return authoritative job snapshot with artifacts, children, and lineage.
List jobs for local operational UI and adapters.
Create or reuse canonical report child job.
Endpoint.rule: Repeated create requests return existing canonical child unless explicit retry is used.

Endpoint.purpose: Create or reuse canonical deep-research child job.

behavior.rule: If job is queued and not claimed, API may finalize directly as canceled.
If job is running, API sets cancel_requested and worker must observe it cooperatively.
If job is terminal, API returns current job unchanged.

Endpoint.purpose: Create new retry row linked by retry_of_job_id.

validation.rule: Only terminal jobs may be retried.
Retry preserves root_job_id and parent_job_id when applicable and reuses source_set_id.

Endpoint.purpose: Resolve artifact metadata plus MinIO presigned download URL.
Endpoint.rule: Clients never construct MinIO URLs themselves.

Endpoint.purpose: Expose append-only event timeline for UI and MCP introspection.

delivery-rules.rule: Webhook is best-effort delivery, not the sole source of truth.
Failed deliveries are retried with bounded backoff and durable state.
Retry on network error, timeout, 408, 429, and 5xx.

SubscriptionModel.default: All connected Web UI clients receive the versioned event stream and filter client-side in local deployment.
SubscriptionModel.v1-rule: No subscribe message exists in v1.

WebSocketContract.MandatoryRule: Polling is the correctness path. WebSocket is a latency optimization. UI must re-fetch job snapshots through REST on version gaps or reconnect.

Lifecycle.step-2: Ask the API or internal service layer to claim the job.
Lifecycle.step-3: If claim fails because the job is already terminal or already owned, stop.
Lifecycle.step-4: Mark job running through the authoritative claim path.
Lifecycle.step-9: Finalize as succeeded, failed, or canceled.

endpoint.behavior: Atomically claims execution, performs queued to running, sets started_at, and returns execution_id, lineage, ordered inputs, params, and version.

endpoint.fields: execution_id and artifacts array with artifact_kind, format, filename, mime_type, object_key, and size_bytes
endpoint.behavior: Upserts artifact metadata by job_id and artifact_kind only after object upload succeeds.

endpoint.fields: execution_id, outcome, progress_stage, progress_message, error_code, error_message
endpoint.behavior: Validates required artifacts on success, writes terminal state, sets finished_at, and emits timeline, WebSocket, and webhook side effects.

TranscriptionWorker.reused-sources: current transcribers.py, transcript rendering in documents.py, source materialization logic from service.py
TranscriptionWorker.progress-stages: queued, materializing_source, concatenating_inputs, transcribing, rendering_artifacts, uploading_artifacts, completed, failed, canceled

ReportWorker.reused-sources: current cglm_runner.py and report docx rendering in documents.py
ReportWorker.progress-stages: queued, loading_transcript, generating_report, rendering_artifacts, uploading_artifacts, completed, failed, canceled

DeepResearchWorker.progress-stages: queued, loading_report, researching, writing_output, uploading_artifacts, completed, failed, canceled

ApiRules.rule-1: When cancel is requested, set cancel_requested_at.
ApiRules.rule-2: If running, set job status to cancel_requested and emit an event.
ApiRules.rule-3: If queued and still not claimed, API may finalize directly as canceled.
ApiRules.rule-4: If job is already terminal, return the current snapshot unchanged.

WorkerRules.rule-4: Check cancellation before artifact upload when skipping is still safe.
WorkerRules.rule-5: Check cancellation inside loops when processing chunked or long-running work.

SubprocessHandling.rule-1: cglm and deep research subprocess handles must be tracked.
SubprocessHandling.rule-2: If cancel is observed, worker should terminate subprocess.
SubprocessHandling.rule-3: If subprocess termination succeeds, finalize as canceled.
SubprocessHandling.rule-4: If subprocess crashes during an authoritative cancel, still report canceled unless corruption requires failed.

RetryPolicy.rule-1: Retry creates a new job row and keeps historical rows immutable.
RetryPolicy.rule-2: New retry row references old row through retry_of_job_id.
RetryPolicy.rule-3: Retry preserves root_job_id and parent_job_id when applicable.
RetryPolicy.rule-5: Retry must not copy artifacts or job events from the source row.
RetryPolicy.rule-6: Create-child operations reuse existing canonical child rows instead of spawning siblings.
RetryPolicy.rule-7: If the caller wants a new report or deep-research run, retry the existing child job instead of calling create-child again.
RetryPolicy.rationale: Retry-as-new-job is cleaner for UI history, debugging, MCP introspection, and event auditability.

WebUi.item: subscribe to WebSocket and fall back to polling
surface delivery strategy choice between polling and webhook on create flows
trigger report and deep-research child jobs

TelegramBot.item: download attachments from Telegram when needed
submit Telegram media groups and explicit combined batches through the combined transcription endpoint
create URL jobs through the API
poll for result readiness by default
send resulting artifacts back to Telegram users
expose buttons that trigger API actions

McpServer.item: expose create, read, list, retry, cancel, report, and deep-research operations
return artifact metadata and download handles

McpServerForbidden.item: talk directly to Redis, MinIO, or workers

WebUi.required-pages: Create Job, Jobs List, Job Details

required-capabilities.item: upload multiple files in one request
create one combined transcription job from multiple files when explicitly selected

late-wave-admin-capabilities.item: re-resolve fresh download link for expired artifacts
manual rerun via explicit retry actions

WebUi.recommended-stack: React, TypeScript, Vite, TanStack Query, React Router

Mcp.recommended-stack: TypeScript, official MCP SDK, generated or thin typed API client from packages/sdk-ts

Telegram.recommended-stack: Python, aiogram, packages/sdk-py or thin internal API client

behavior-mapping.map: plain transcript ready to send plain transcript artifact
segmented transcript button to resolve and send segmented artifact
report button to create report job and wait or poll
deep research button to create deep-research job and wait or poll

runtime-policy.rule: Telegram bot does not expose advanced per-job runtime knobs in v1.
Bot chooses its delivery strategy explicitly per request and defaults to polling.
Telegram media groups map automatically to the combined transcription flow; sequential standalone media use the combined flow only after an explicit user action such as Обработать вместе.

service.name: postgres
service.notes: local persistent volume and initialized database

service.name: redis
service.notes: no persistence requirement beyond local convenience

service.name: minio
service.notes: local persistent volume plus published S3 endpoint and published admin console on localhost

service.name: api
service.notes: depends on postgres, redis, minio; exposes HTTP and WebSocket; owns webhook delivery dispatcher

service.name: worker-transcription
service.notes: depends on api, redis, minio; includes ffmpeg and faster-whisper

CpuNotes.note-1: Do not start multiple transcription workers by default if CPU is constrained.
CpuNotes.note-2: Keep report and deep-research workers separate so they do not block the transcription queue.
CpuNotes.note-3: Surface long-running progress in API and WebSocket so UI does not appear frozen.

EnvironmentContracts.SharedInfrastructure: POSTGRES_DSN, REDIS_URL, MINIO_ENDPOINT, MINIO_ACCESS_KEY, MINIO_SECRET_KEY, MINIO_BUCKET_SOURCES, MINIO_BUCKET_ARTIFACTS
EnvironmentContracts.Api: API_BIND_ADDR, API_BASE_URL, WS_BASE_URL, MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_BYTES, WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_ENABLED, WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_MAX_ATTEMPTS, WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_BACKOFF_SECONDS
EnvironmentContracts.TranscriptionWorker: WHISPER_MODEL, WHISPER_DEVICE equals cpu, WHISPER_COMPUTE_TYPE, FFMPEG_BIN

Map.to: execution-specific code into Python workers; orchestration semantics into Go API; metadata persistence replaced by PostgreSQL, MinIO, and Redis integration
message parsing, Telegram downloads, client-side polling or wait flow, result delivery, and callback-to-API mapping only

MigrationMapping.MovedOutFromBot: direct execution, local job state persistence, direct report generation, deep research execution

contract.purpose: Provide the canonical API, WebSocket, webhook, enum, and validation contracts for the entire platform.
contract.scope: OpenAPI routes, shared schemas, public enums, webhook envelopes, WebSocket envelopes, and internal worker DTOs.

export-openapi.PURPOSE: Describe public and internal HTTP routes.

export-validationSchemas.PURPOSE: Describe request and response validation surfaces.

notes.note-1: This module must be complete before the first large implementation wave.

contract.purpose: Own PostgreSQL persistence, MinIO access, presigned URL resolution, and durable webhook-delivery records.
contract.scope: SQL persistence, MinIO object IO, presigned URL generation, source and artifact lifecycle checks, and durable delivery bookkeeping.
contract.links: M-API-STORAGE, V-M-API-STORAGE, DF-001, DF-003, DF-006, DF-007

export-persistJob.PURPOSE: Create and transition jobs with versioned state.

export-resolveArtifact.PURPOSE: Return artifact metadata plus a fresh presigned URL.

notes.note-1: job_submissions and webhook_deliveries are mandatory early tables, not optional polish.

contract.purpose: Publish typed asynq tasks and encode queue-level retry and timeout defaults without owning business semantics.
contract.scope: Task type mapping, payload shaping, timeout policy, max retry settings, and SkipRetry classification.
contract.links: M-API-QUEUE, V-M-API-QUEUE, DF-001, DF-004, DF-005, DF-006, DF-007

export-enqueue.PURPOSE: Publish job:transcription.run, job:report.run, and job:deep_research.run tasks.

notes.note-1: Business-visible retry remains API-owned even though asynq performs transport-level retries.

contract.purpose: Emit authoritative job events to job timelines, WebSocket clients, and webhook deliveries.
contract.scope: Versioned event persistence, WebSocket fanout, webhook scheduling, and reconnect-safe event semantics.

export-emitJobEvent.PURPOSE: Persist and fan out versioned job events.

notes.note-1: WebSocket is a low-latency UI channel; polling remains the correctness path.

contract.purpose: Enforce delivery strategy, idempotency, job lineage, child reuse, cancel, retry, and terminal-state invariants.
contract.scope: Root job creation, child-job creation, state transitions, version increments, retry lineage, cancel semantics, and idempotency enforcement.
contract.links: M-API-JOBS, V-M-API-JOBS, DF-001, DF-002, DF-004, DF-005, DF-006, DF-007

export-createTranscriptionJobs.PURPOSE: Create one job per uploaded file or one job from a supported URL.

export-createChildJob.PURPOSE: Create or reuse canonical report or deep-research child jobs.

export-retryJob.PURPOSE: Create a new lineage-linked retry row.

notes.note-1: Workers must never bypass this module’s transition rules.

contract.purpose: Expose public and internal HTTP routes that bind incoming requests to API job orchestration logic.
contract.scope: Multipart upload handling, JSON request validation, route binding, error envelopes, and WebSocket session entry.
contract.links: M-API-HTTP, V-M-API-HTTP, DF-001, DF-002, DF-003, DF-004, DF-005, DF-006, DF-007

export-handleWebsocket.PURPOSE: Accept WebSocket connections for UI live updates.

notes.note-1: Public routes and internal worker routes should remain explicit rather than merged by convention.

contract.purpose: Provide shared Python helpers for claim, progress, finalize calls, storage utilities, and execution scaffolding.
contract.scope: Internal API client helpers, artifact upload helpers, typed execution context parsing, and common cancellation probes.
contract.links: M-WORKER-COMMON, V-M-WORKER-COMMON, DF-001, DF-004, DF-005, DF-006, DF-007

notes.note-1: Keep worker-to-API DTOs stable and versioned through M-CONTRACTS.

contract.purpose: Reuse source extraction, YouTube resolution, faster-whisper, and document rendering to produce transcript artifacts.
contract.scope: Source materialization, subtitle fast path, Whisper fallback, transcript rendering, artifact upload, and terminal finalization.
contract.links: M-WORKER-TRANSCRIPTION, V-M-WORKER-TRANSCRIPTION, DF-001, DF-002, DF-006, DF-007

notes.note-1: This module reuses current Python execution logic instead of rewriting ML behavior in Go.

contract.purpose: Generate markdown and DOCX research reports from transcript artifacts using cglm.
contract.scope: cglm command execution, subprocess lifecycle handling, report rendering, artifact upload, and terminal finalization.

notes.note-1: Cancellation must be subprocess-aware because cglm runs out of process.

contract.purpose: Run the evidence-driven deep-research pipeline and persist the final markdown output plus optional execution logs.
contract.scope: Phase-driven deep-research execution, required artifact validation, optional log persistence, and terminal finalization.

notes.note-1: Deep-research phase gates and required artifacts must remain deterministic under the new job shell.

contract.purpose: Expose the local browser UI for submission, monitoring, artifact download, and late-wave admin operations.
contract.scope: Jobs list, job details, uploads, URL submission, artifact resolution, polling and WS reconciliation, and late-wave admin actions.
contract.links: M-WEB-UI, V-M-WEB-UI, DF-001, DF-003, DF-006, DF-007

export-jobDetails.PURPOSE: Show lineage, status, artifacts, and admin actions.

notes.note-1: Polling is the truth path; WebSocket is only the latency optimization path.

contract.purpose: Preserve Telegram UX while delegating all business logic to the API.
contract.scope: Telegram update parsing, temporary media download, API submission, polling wait flow, callback mapping, and artifact sending.
contract.links: M-TELEGRAM-ADAPTER, V-M-TELEGRAM-ADAPTER, DF-001, DF-004, DF-005, DF-007

export-handleMedia.PURPOSE: Upload Telegram media to the API and send resulting artifacts back.

notes.note-1: Default delivery strategy is polling for Telegram flows.

contract.purpose: Expose the platform as an MCP surface over the same typed HTTP API client.
contract.scope: MCP tool definitions, API request mapping, result shaping, and tool-level read or write parity with the HTTP API.
contract.links: M-MCP-ADAPTER, V-M-MCP-ADAPTER, DF-001, DF-004, DF-005, DF-007

export-readJob.PURPOSE: Inspect jobs, artifacts, and event timelines through MCP.

notes.note-1: MCP remains a thin adapter and must not gain direct DB, queue, or MinIO access.

M-INFRA-COMPOSE.NAME: InfraCompose

contract.purpose: Own local Docker Compose topology, service dependencies, and environment contracts.
contract.scope: Compose topology, healthchecks, startup ordering, volumes, service env contracts, and smoke-run expectations.
contract.links: M-INFRA-COMPOSE, V-M-INFRA-COMPOSE, DF-001, DF-003, DF-006, DF-007

export-composeStack.PURPOSE: Start and verify the local platform stack.

M-INFRA-COMPOSE.depends: M-API-HTTP, M-WORKER-TRANSCRIPTION, M-WORKER-REPORT, M-WORKER-DEEP-RESEARCH, M-WEB-UI, M-TELEGRAM-ADAPTER, M-MCP-ADAPTER

notes.note-1: Healthchecks are required before cutover because there is no long-lived legacy fallback track.

DF-001.NAME: UploadedFileTranscription
DF-001.step-1: Client uploads one or more files to the API with delivery strategy defaulting to polling unless webhook is selected.
DF-001.step-2: API stores source files in MinIO, persists job_submissions, sources, and jobs, then enqueues one transcription task per file.
DF-001.step-3: Transcription worker claims the job, materializes source input, runs transcription, renders artifacts, uploads them, and finalizes the job.
DF-001.step-4: API emits versioned events, exposes polling reads, and schedules webhook deliveries only for jobs whose delivery strategy is webhook.

DF-007.NAME: CombinedUploadedMediaTranscription
DF-007.step-1: Client uploads one or more files to the combined endpoint with delivery strategy defaulting to polling unless webhook is selected.
DF-007.step-2: API stores each source file in MinIO, persists one ordered source set plus its ordered source_set_items, then creates one transcription job bound to that source set.
DF-007.step-3: Transcription worker claims the job, materializes all ordered inputs, normalizes them to a common audio representation, concatenates them in temp space, runs one transcription pass, renders one artifact set, uploads it, and finalizes the job.
DF-007.step-4: API emits versioned events, exposes one authoritative polling snapshot, and schedules webhook deliveries only when the combined job uses webhook delivery.

DF-002.NAME: YouTubeUrlToTranscript
DF-002.step-1: Client submits a supported YouTube URL to the API.
DF-002.step-2: API validates the URL host, persists source metadata, creates a transcription job, and enqueues it.
DF-002.step-3: Worker resolves transcript or media through YouTube adapters, generates transcript artifacts, and finalizes the job.

DF-003.NAME: VersionedEventDelivery
DF-003.TRIGGER: job state transition or artifact creation
DF-003.step-1: API writes a new job_events row whenever job version advances or an artifact is created.
DF-003.step-2: Web UI receives low-latency updates through WebSocket and reconciles with REST on reconnect or version gaps.
DF-003.step-3: If the job uses webhook, API persists a webhook_deliveries row and retries bounded delivery until success or dead state.

DF-004.NAME: TranscriptToReport
DF-004.step-1: API validates that the parent transcription job succeeded and contains transcript_plain.
DF-004.step-2: API reuses an existing canonical report child or creates a new report job and enqueues it.
DF-004.step-3: Report worker runs cglm, renders report artifacts, uploads them, and finalizes the report job.

DF-005.NAME: ReportToDeepResearch
DF-005.step-1: API validates that the parent report job succeeded and contains report_markdown.
DF-005.step-2: API reuses an existing canonical deep-research child or creates a new deep-research job and enqueues it.
DF-005.step-3: Deep-research worker runs the evidence-driven pipeline, uploads final markdown and optional logs, and finalizes the job.

DF-006.NAME: CancelOrRetry
DF-006.step-1: API validates current job state and either finalizes queued jobs as canceled or marks running jobs as cancel_requested.
DF-006.step-2: Workers check cancellation between expensive phases and finalize as canceled when authoritative cancellation is observed.
DF-006.step-3: Retry creates a new lineage-linked job row and never mutates historical execution records.

Wave-0.name: Baseline Freeze And Acceptance SSOT
Wave-0.goal: Freeze current behavior so the migration has a hard acceptance target.
Wave-0.Phase-0-1: Record current supported flows: YouTube, Telegram voice, Telegram audio or video or document, media groups, plain transcript, segmented transcript, report, deep research.
Wave-0.Phase-0-2: Record current artifacts transcript.txt, transcript.md, transcript.docx, report.md, report.docx, and deep research final report.
Wave-0.Phase-0-3: Freeze acceptance tests and identify which current tests must remain conceptually valid after migration.
Wave-0.Phase-0-4: Freeze newly accepted architectural decisions including asynq, polling plus webhook strategy model, MinIO presigned downloads, multi-file upload, no legacy compatibility track, and late-wave admin operations.

Wave-1.name: Monorepo Bootstrap And Contracts
Wave-1.goal: Create the monorepo structure and contract package before implementation spreads.
Wave-1.Phase-1-1: Create repository structure and placeholder READMEs.
Wave-1.Phase-1-2: Write OpenAPI draft for create transcription job, get job, list jobs, create report job, create deep-research job, cancel, retry, artifact resolve endpoint, idempotency, delivery strategy and webhook fields, and multi-file upload shapes.
Wave-1.Phase-1-2a: Freeze the separate combined transcription endpoint and source-set semantics without changing the existing one-file-to-one-job upload contract.
Wave-1.Phase-1-3: Write WebSocket schema draft with event envelope and event types.
Wave-1.Phase-1-3a: Write webhook schema draft with envelope, retry semantics, lifecycle events, and failure handling.
Wave-1.Phase-1-4: Define shared enums for job types, statuses, artifact kinds, and source kinds.

Wave-2.name: Infrastructure And Go API Skeleton
Wave-2.goal: Bring up local infra and a compilable API skeleton with DB and queue wiring.
Wave-2.Phase-2-1: Add PostgreSQL migrations for sources, source_sets, source_set_items, jobs, job_artifacts, job_events, job_submissions, and webhook_deliveries.
Wave-2.Phase-2-2: Add MinIO integration and bucket bootstrap assumptions.
Wave-2.Phase-2-3: Add Redis queue producer through asynq by job type.
Wave-2.Phase-2-4: Add REST endpoints as stubs first and then as persistence-backed handlers.
Wave-2.Phase-2-5: Add WebSocket server with connection, event broadcast, and reconnect-safe snapshot rule.
Wave-2.Phase-2-6: Add webhook delivery subsystem with stored webhook metadata, background delivery loop, bounded retry, and event emission rules per job type.

Wave-3.name: Transcription Worker Extraction
Wave-3.goal: Move transcription execution out of the monolithic bot into a standalone worker.
Wave-3.Phase-3-1: Extract reusable Python execution library by moving transcription and document rendering logic into worker-common.
Wave-3.Phase-3-2: Implement worker claim and run loop: receive job_id, fetch ordered input metadata, download one or more sources from MinIO if needed, concatenate ordered media when the source set has multiple items, execute transcription, upload artifacts, publish progress, finalize job through the API-owned control contract.
Wave-3.Phase-3-3: Preserve current output formats: plain transcript, segmented markdown transcript, transcript docx.
Wave-3.Phase-3-4: Add cancellation integration by checking the cancel flag before and after long work.

Wave-4.name: Report Worker Extraction
Wave-4.goal: Split report generation into a dedicated job type and worker.
Wave-4.Phase-4-1: Extract report generation package by moving cglm_runner and report DOCX generation into report worker layer.
Wave-4.Phase-4-2: Implement report job creation in API with validation that parent transcription artifacts exist.
Wave-4.Phase-4-3: Implement report worker lifecycle: claim report job, read transcript artifact, run cglm, upload markdown and docx, finalize.

Wave-5.name: Deep Research Worker Extraction
Wave-5.goal: Split deep research into a dedicated job type and worker.
Wave-5.Phase-5-1: Extract deep research package with minimal rewrite.
Wave-5.Phase-5-2: Implement API deep-research child-job creation with report artifact validation.
Wave-5.Phase-5-3: Implement cooperative cancellation around each phase and subprocess execution.

Wave-6.name: Web UI Delivery
Wave-6.goal: Deliver a simple operational React UI over the live API.
Wave-6.Phase-6-1: Scaffold app shell with routes, layout, and API client wiring.
Wave-6.Phase-6-2: Create jobs list page displaying job type, status, created time, and progress.
Wave-6.Phase-6-3: Create job details page showing status, progress, event timeline, artifacts, and child jobs.
Wave-6.Phase-6-4: Add create job form supporting per-file upload, explicit combined multi-media upload, and YouTube URL submission.
Wave-6.Phase-6-5: Add polling and WebSocket integration using polling as fallback and WebSocket for fast updates.
Wave-6.Phase-6-6: Add actions for report, deep research, cancel, and retry.
Wave-6.Phase-6-7: Add delivery strategy controls with default polling, required webhook URL validation, and explicit explanation that each job chooses exactly one strategy: polling or webhook.
Wave-6.Phase-6-8: Add late-wave admin operations on job details page: delete job, delete artifacts, refresh expired artifact download link, explicit manual retry actions.

Wave-7.name: Telegram Bot Migration
Wave-7.goal: Replace direct execution in Telegram bot with API calls.
Wave-7.Phase-7-1: Add typed Python API client supporting create upload job, create combined upload job, create URL job, get job, list jobs, create report job, create deep-research job, cancel, retry, and artifact resolution.
Wave-7.Phase-7-2: Adapt message flow so the bot becomes an input parser, Telegram downloader, HTTP client, and artifact sender. Use explicit delivery strategy per request and default to polling. Map Telegram media groups to the combined endpoint and allow explicit buffered combined submission for sequential media.
Wave-7.Phase-7-3: Preserve current UX: plain transcript immediate delivery, segmented transcript button, report button, deep research button.
Wave-7.Phase-7-4: Remove execution coupling so the bot no longer imports execution core directly.

Wave-8.name: MCP Server Delivery
Wave-8.goal: Expose the platform to MCP as a clean adapter.
Wave-8.Phase-8-2: Add core tools for create transcription job, get job, list jobs, create report job, create deep-research job, cancel, and retry.
Wave-8.Phase-8-3: Add artifact visibility through artifact metadata and download endpoints.

Wave-9.name: Compose Hardening And Cutover
Wave-9.goal: Make the new platform the default local runtime.
Wave-9.Phase-9-1: Add persistent volumes and init scripts.
Wave-9.Phase-9-3: Add local runbook documenting startup, restart, logs, and common failures.
Wave-9.Phase-9-4: Cut over old single-process entrypoint after compose smoke verification and functional parity checks pass.

Phase-1.name: Grace Foundation And Contract Freeze
Phase-1.goal: Prepare the repository for GRACE-driven implementation and freeze contracts before any large code move.
Phase-1.step-1: Convert the migration plan into canonical OpenAPI, JSON schema, WebSocket, and webhook contracts.
Phase-1.step-2: Define required runtime topology, services, and health expectations in a GRACE-governed shape.

Phase-2.name: Control Plane Foundation
Phase-2.goal: Build the minimum Go control plane that can persist jobs, enqueue work, and expose contract-compliant HTTP routes.
Phase-2.step-1: Add PostgreSQL migrations and MinIO integration.
Phase-2.step-2: Add asynq task publishing and retry policy wiring.
Phase-2.step-3: Implement job creation, child reuse, cancel, retry, and lineage rules.
Phase-2.step-4: Implement job events, WebSocket fanout, and persisted webhook delivery scheduling.
Phase-2.step-5: Expose public and internal worker routes.

Phase-3.name: Transcription Worker Extraction
Phase-3.goal: Extract the current transcription path into a standalone worker without changing user-visible artifact semantics.
Phase-3.step-1: Create shared Python worker API and storage helpers.
Phase-3.step-2: Move current transcription execution and transcript rendering into worker form.

Phase-4.name: Report And Deep Research Extraction
Phase-4.goal: Split report and deep-research execution into dedicated workers with stable child-job contracts.
Phase-4.step-1: Extract report generation through cglm into the report worker.
Phase-4.step-2: Extract deep-research pipeline into the dedicated worker.

Phase-5.name: Adapter And UI Cutover
Phase-5.goal: Move all user-facing entrypoints to thin adapters over the API.
Phase-5.step-1: Implement local job submission, monitoring, artifact download, and admin details pages.
Phase-5.step-2: Replace bot-side orchestration with API calls while preserving Telegram UX.
Phase-5.step-3: Expose the same core operations through MCP.

Phase-6.name: Compose Hardening And Final Cutover
Phase-6.goal: Validate the full local stack, enforce health gates, and remove the old single-process entrypoint.
Phase-6.step-1: Harden compose startup order, healthchecks, volumes, and smoke scripts.
Phase-6.step-2: Verify full-stack parity against the frozen acceptance matrix before deleting the old runtime path.

risk.name: Rewriting too much execution logic
risk.mitigation: Keep the Python execution plane, move code instead of rewriting for style, and introduce wrappers rather than reinventions.

risk.name: Premature Go rewrite of ML or report logic
risk.mitigation: Go owns the control plane only; workers stay Python.

risk.name: WebSocket state drift
risk.mitigation: Polling remains authoritative fallback and jobs carry a monotonic version field.

risk.name: Cancellation is inconsistent across job types
risk.mitigation: Define cooperative cancellation checkpoints per worker and test subprocess termination explicitly.

risk.name: Compose stack becomes fragile
risk.mitigation: Add healthchecks, startup order, runbook documentation, and keep local deployment assumptions simple.

risk.name: Big-bang cutover increases integration risk
risk.mitigation: Freeze behavior baseline early, move UX only after API and workers are correct, and require compose-wide smoke verification before replacing the old entrypoint.

DefinitionOfDone.criterion: API server owns all business logic orchestration.
Transcription, report, and deep research run only in workers.
Telegram bot is a thin API client.
MCP server is a thin API client.
Web UI can create, observe, and manage jobs.
PostgreSQL is authoritative job state storage.
MinIO stores source files and artifacts.
Redis-backed asynq is used for queueing, while event delivery is driven from persisted job events inside the API.
Polling and WebSocket are both implemented.
Webhook callback mode is implemented for all job types.
Cancellation works across all job types.
Artifact downloads resolve to valid presigned MinIO links.
Multi-file upload through the API works.
Admin operations on the web details page exist in the planned late-wave scope.
Docker Compose can bring up the full platform locally.

ImmediateNextTask.historical-guidance: Implementation should start with Wave 0 and Wave 1 only. Do not begin with UI or Telegram refactor first.

recommended-order.item: add DB schema and asynq producer
only then add UI and adapter clients

ExecutionRules.rule: Do not start from the UI.
Do not start from the MCP server.
Do not rewrite Python execution into Go.
Do not add auth or quota logic.
Do not build direct client-to-MinIO uploads in v1.
Do not use WebSocket as the only status mechanism.
Do not expose arbitrary runtime tuning knobs in Web UI or Telegram bot in v1.
Do not treat webhook delivery as stronger than polling.
Do not implement retry by mutating a finished job row.
ExecutionRules.separation: Go API equals control plane; Python workers equal execution plane; UI, Telegram bot, and MCP equal adapters.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope for the active module packet.
